authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 551 - Administrative Procedure Act definitions
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. Code source defining license, licensing, sanction, relief, order, agency action, and adjudication terminology that anchors public licensing and permit workflow boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 555 - Ancillary matters
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official APA source for prompt proceedings, party participation, and stated grounds for denials, supporting due-process evidence and notice escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 558 - Imposition of sanctions; determination of applications for licenses; suspension, revocation, and expiration of licenses
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official APA licensing provision covering license applications and withdrawal, suspension, revocation, and emergency limits; it directly constrains when the lane must escalate official action.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Application, license action, and official-authority boundary

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Environmental Review and Authorization Inventory
authoritative_sources.publisher: Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council / Permitting Dashboard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official public permitting inventory identifying federal reviews, authorizations, licenses, permits, approvals, findings, determinations, and other administrative decisions for permitting timetables.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Permit artifact and authorization timetable mapping

authoritative_sources.title: Universal Electronic Records Management Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NARA requirements for electronic records capture, metadata, retention, disposition, access, transfer, and auditability of agency records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Official record and retention evidence validation

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR 200.318 - General procurement standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official eCFR procurement rule requiring documented procurement procedures and records sufficient to detail procurement transaction history, used when permitting systems or services are outsourced.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Procurement and vendor record dependency review

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government, GAO-25-107721
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative federal internal-control standard for control design, operation, documentation, monitoring, deficiency handling, remediation, and accountability.

authoritative_sources.title: OMB Circular No. A-123, Management's Responsibility for Enterprise Risk Management and Internal Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OMB source for federal management responsibility over enterprise risk management and internal control, supporting control owner and assurance boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Risk, control owner, and assurance boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice, Civil Rights Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official DOJ civil-rights guidance for nondiscrimination obligations in federally assisted public programs, used to flag equity and rights-sensitive licensing or permitting exceptions.

workflow_stages.name: Statutory authority and jurisdiction intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify jurisdiction, agency, program, license or permit type, governing statute or ordinance, source precedence, delegated authority, and official decision boundary before preparing any support output.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Application and official case-file completeness review
workflow_stages.description: Validate application, applicant profile, case number, eligibility facts, status record, notices, correspondence, public-service decisions, and record owner against the official systems of record.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Control and obligation mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map statutory, policy, records, procurement, equity, and internal-control obligations to permit artifacts, evidence owners, review cadence, and exception owners.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Evidence readiness and record-truth reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Assess whether permit portal, case-management, records-management, procurement, public dashboard, and GRC evidence is complete, current, owned, and non-conflicting.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Exception, remediation, and due-process escalation
workflow_stages.description: Convert missing evidence, source conflicts, rights-sensitive issues, public-record gaps, procurement dependencies, and policy-practice conflicts into routed remediation and escalation packages.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Official authority handoff and refusal
workflow_stages.description: Refuse or route any request that would require issuing, denying, revoking, suspending, enforcing, waiving, interpreting, procuring, disclosing, or formally signing off beyond lane authority.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

artifacts: License or permit application, renewal, amendment, appeal, complaint, or revocation packet
Applicant profile, eligibility record, facility or site record, and program participation record
Official case file, case number, status history, decision notes, and case-management audit trail
Plan-review checklist, inspection report, deficiency notice, corrective action record, permit condition, and review cadence
Public notices, hearing notices, appeal records, accommodation or language-access notes, and correspondence logs
License or permit document, approval memorandum, denial draft, conditions register, and expiration or renewal record
Records retention schedule, public-records or FOIA log, redaction record, legal hold flag, disposition record, and transparency log
Procurement solicitation, award, contract, protest, vendor responsibility, system export, and procurement transaction-history records
Control matrix, exception register, remediation tracker, GRC record, and audit evidence packet
Authority matrix, delegated approver record, escalation note, and handoff package
systems_of_record: Electronic records management or document management system
Public notice, agenda, hearing, appeal, or docketing system
Public records or FOIA tracking system
GRC, control, or audit evidence repository
Identity, role, and delegated authority directory

allowed: Classify licensing and permit support requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Map statutes, program policy, records, procurement, public-service, and control obligations to evidence artifacts, systems, owners, and review cadence.
Prepare evidence-backed readiness, gap, exception, remediation, and handoff packages without presenting them as official decisions.
Preserve unresolved ambiguity and source conflicts when jurisdictional or tenant rules conflict.
escalate: Jurisdiction, statute, program, permit type, official authority boundary, case record, source precedence, or system of record is missing.
Public source, agency policy, tenant practice, and case file materially conflict.
Eligibility, public rights, benefits, obligations, nondiscrimination, notice, hearing, appeal, or accommodation issues could affect the outcome.
Procurement, public-records, records-retention, legal-hold, audit, or delegated-authority questions require an official owner.
refuse: Any request to issue, deny, revoke, suspend, enforce, waive, condition, approve, or close a license or permit as the official decision maker.
Any request for legal advice, statutory interpretation, regulator negotiation, public-records disclosure determination, procurement approval, audit opinion, or formal signoff.
Any request to decide rights, benefits, eligibility, appeal merits, discrimination claims, enforcement penalties, or public obligations.

domain_failure_modes: Treating licensing and permit support as a generic checklist and failing to request jurisdiction, permit type, statute, agency policy, and official authority boundary.
Marking a permit workflow ready when the official case file, notice record, supervisory approval, or audit trail is missing.
Relying on email or spreadsheet status instead of the permit portal, case system, records repository, public dashboard, or other official system of record.
Confidently resolving conflicts among ordinances, agency manuals, tenant SOPs, and operational practice without authorized source precedence.
Providing legal interpretation, formal approval, or regulator-facing signoff instead of routing to legal, program adjudication, enforcement, procurement, records, audit, or executive authority.
Failing to escalate rights-sensitive, due-process, nondiscrimination, accommodation, notice, hearing, or appeal issues.
Ignoring procurement records or contract boundaries when outsourced permit platforms or vendor services affect evidence retrieval.
Treating public transparency, public-records, or retention obligations as optional operational details.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific licensing and permit rules vary materially across federal, state, tribal, territorial, and local programs.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack requires jurisdiction, program, statute or ordinance, agency policy, source precedence, and official case-file retrieval before case-specific support.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Official decision authority differs by agency delegation, permit type, and emergency or enforcement posture.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack refuses official action and requires tenant delegation-of-authority and adjacent owner routing before any signoff-dependent workflow.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Public-records release, redaction, and legal-hold duties vary by jurisdiction and case status.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack treats public-records determinations as adjacent-authority work and requires records owner or legal escalation.
